summary: High‑fidelity soundboard capture of a stellar late‑90s set highlighted by a soaring Uncle John’s Band and stratospheric jams.
review: The recording is a clean, well‑balanced soundboard source that lets listeners hear every nuance of what many fans describe as one of the best mid‑90s Dead shows.  Highlights include an epic Uncle John’s Band, a blazing China Cat Sunflower → I Need a Miracle → The Rider sequence and a majestic Dark Star → Stella Blue outing, all propelled by Jerry’s inspired playing and Vince’s freshly found keyboard voice.

ai_rating.rationale: Three enthusiastic 5‑star reviews praise the musical performance; the single technical comment rates the soundboard quality as solid, yielding an overall rating in the excellent range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Soundboard source with clear, well‑balanced mix; reviewers note a classic ’90s‑era fidelity and report no significant dropouts or muddiness.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Need a Miracle > The Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong first set builds energy with Jack Straw, Spoonful and a fiery Friend of the Devil; the second set soars through seamless transitions, culminating in epic jams that keep the momentum high throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as rebounding strongly, delivering soaring leads and beautiful peaks despite a rough voice and a single vocal flub.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for his “icing on the cake” Box of Rain performance.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for his continued love of Dylan songs and an epic Take Me to a Good Time (Deal) contribution.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince is highlighted as having fully found his place, delivering great work on Deal and the China Cat Sunflower sequence.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drum > Space segment in the first set is singled out as a standout moment.
